full_response:
We want the scalar obtained by contracting a symmetric rank–2 tensor with an antisymmetric rank–2 tensor.

Definitions  
Symmetrization: T_{(ab)} = ½ (T_{ab}+T_{ba}), so T_{(ab)} = T_{(ba)}.  
Antisymmetrization: ω^{[ab]} = ½ (ω^{ab}-ω^{ba}), so ω^{[ab]} = -ω^{[ba]}.

Compute the contraction:

T_{(ab)} ω^{[ab]}
= ½·½ (T_{ab}+T_{ba})(ω^{ab}-ω^{ba})
= ¼ [T_{ab}ω^{ab} - T_{ab}ω^{ba} + T_{ba}ω^{ab} - T_{ba}ω^{ba}].

Relabel dummy indices in the last two terms: interchange a↔b in both T_{ba}ω^{ab} and T_{ba}ω^{ba}; since dummy indices are summed, this leaves the value unchanged but makes the pattern identical to the first two terms:

T_{ba}ω^{ab} → T_{ab}ω^{ba},  
T_{ba}ω^{ba} → T_{ab}ω^{ab}.

Hence the expression becomes

¼ [T_{ab}ω^{ab} - T_{ab}ω^{ba} + T_{ab}ω^{ba} - T_{ab}ω^{ab}] = 0.

More compactly, for any symmetric S_{ab} (S_{ab}=S_{ba}) and antisymmetric A^{ab} (A^{ab}=-A^{ba}):

S_{ab}A^{ab} = -S_{ba}A^{ab} = -S_{ab}A^{ab} ⇒ S_{ab}A^{ab}=0.

Therefore the contraction of a symmetric with an antisymmetric tensor always vanishes.

\boxed{\,T_{(ab)}\;\omega^{[ab]} = 0\,}

Confidence: 97%
